data.headline: Bathroom Remodeling in Branford, CT
data.subheadline: Licensed Connecticut bathroom remodeling contractor serving Branford and New Haven County — complete renovations from walk-in showers to full master suites.

data.title: Bathroom Remodeling in Branford, CT
data.content:
Branford's shoreline and inland neighborhoods feature bathrooms that reflect their era — dated tile, worn fixtures, and layouts that need modernizing. Shoreline properties near Stony Creek and Indian Neck present coastal moisture considerations, while inland neighborhoods share standard mid-century conditions.

BuiltWell provides bathroom remodeling across all Branford neighborhoods, with material and waterproofing specifications tailored to each property's location and conditions.

data.title: What Is Included in a Bathroom Remodel in Branford
data.content:
A full bathroom remodel in Branford covers every trade from demolition through final paint: tile, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, vanity and countertop installation, shower or tub configuration, glass enclosures, fixtures, hardware, ventilation, and painting.

We start with careful demolition and removal of all existing tile, fixtures, and vanities. Before any new work begins, we assess what's behind the walls — Branford homes frequently have moisture damage, deteriorated plumbing, inadequate waterproofing, or structural conditions that need to be addressed before the renovation can proceed properly.

Waterproofing is critical in every bathroom renovation. We use Kerdi membrane and cement board throughout all wet areas — shower walls, shower floors, tub surrounds, and any surface that will receive tile in a wet environment. This prevents the moisture damage that causes most bathroom renovations to fail prematurely.

Tile installation includes shower walls, floors, niches, and accent areas. Vanity installation includes countertop, sink, and all plumbing connections. Electrical scope covers GFCI outlets, exhaust fan upgrades, vanity lighting, and heated floor systems. All projects include painting, trim work, hardware installation, and a detailed final walkthrough.

data.title: What Makes Bathroom Remodeling in Branford Different
data.content:
Branford's mix of shoreline and inland properties means bathroom renovation approaches differ by location. Shoreline bathrooms require enhanced waterproofing and moisture-resistant materials. Inland bathrooms present standard mid-century conditions.

Stony Creek and Indian Neck properties may have crawl spaces or basements with elevated moisture levels that affect ground-floor bathroom conditions. We assess every Branford property's moisture environment before finalizing specifications.

data.title: Branford Building Department and Permits
data.content: The Branford Building Department handles permits efficiently. Bathroom renovations involving plumbing, electrical, or structural work require permits. Coastal properties may have additional requirements. We handle all applications and inspections.

rows.label: Half Bath Update
rows.notes: New vanity, toilet, flooring, paint, and lighting

rows.notes: New tile, vanity, tub or shower, plumbing, and electrical

data.title: Bathroom Remodeling Cost in Branford, CT
data.subtitle: Bathroom remodeling costs in Branford depend on the scope of work, material selections, and the condition of existing plumbing and structure. Ranges are illustrative for typical Connecticut projects. Final pricing depends on scope, materials, site conditions, and permit fees. Detailed written estimate provided before any work begins.

data.title: Why Bathroom Remodeling in Branford Requires Local Expertise
data.content:
Branford homes present bathroom conditions that contractors without local experience regularly miss.

Branford shoreline bathrooms require enhanced waterproofing — Kerdi membrane, cement board, and proper ventilation are essential in coastal environments where ambient humidity stays elevated.

Inland Branford homes present standard mid-century conditions: original plumbing, dated tile, and bathrooms that haven't been updated in 40+ years. We handle both environments with specifications tailored to each.

data.title: Our Bathroom Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every bathroom remodel follows the same five-step process — structured to keep projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.description: We visit your home, assess the existing bathroom conditions — plumbing, electrical, ventilation, structural — and discuss your goals. No charge. No obligation. We identify potential issues upfront so there are no surprises during construction.

steps.title: Planning
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering every trade: tile, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, vanity, fixtures, glass, and paint. Each line item is broken out so you understand exactly where the budget is going. We include a complete project schedule.

steps.title: Selections
steps.description: We guide you through tile, vanity, fixture, and hardware selections with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so everything arrives before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. Demolition, rough plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, tile, vanity installation, fixture connections, and paint — all sequenced properly. You receive daily updates and a clean, dust-controlled workspace.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished bathroom together — every tile, every grout line, every fixture, every piece of hardware. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete.

data.title: Project Timeline
data.subtitle: Most bathroom remodels take three to eight weeks from demolition to final walkthrough.

items.title: Planning & Design
items.description: 1–2 Weeks. Consultation, measurements, material selections, and detailed proposal.

items.title: Material Lead Time
items.description: 1–4 Weeks. Vanity, tile, fixtures, and glass enclosure ordering and delivery.

items.title: Construction
items.description: 2–4 Weeks. Demo, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, tile, vanity, fixtures, and paint.

items.title: Final Touches
items.description: 2–3 Days. Hardware, accessories, glass installation, cleanup, and walkthrough.

items.question: How long does a bathroom remodel take in Branford?
items.answer: Most Branford bathroom remodels take three to eight weeks of active construction, plus material lead time. A hall bath typically takes 3-4 weeks. A full master bath remodel takes 5-8 weeks. Your written proposal includes a complete project schedule that accounts for all lead times.

items.question: How much does bathroom remodeling cost in Branford?
items.answer: Bathroom remodeling in Branford ranges from $8,000 for a half-bath update to $100,000+ for a luxury master suite with premium stone, radiant heat, and custom features. A standard hall bath remodel typically falls between $15,000 and $30,000. A master bath with walk-in shower and double vanity runs $30,000 to $60,000. Your written proposal includes a detailed cost breakdown by trade.

items.question: Do I need a permit for bathroom remodeling in Branford?
items.answer: Yes. Most bathroom remodeling projects in Branford require permits from the Branford Building Department, particularly for plumbing and electrical work. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project.

items.question: What types of bathrooms do you remodel in Branford?
items.answer: We handle all bathroom types in Branford: half baths, hall baths, master baths, guest baths, and basement bathrooms. Branford's housing stock includes colonial, cape, and ranch-style homes. We're familiar with the construction conditions in these homes and account for them in every proposal.

items.question: Can you convert my tub to a walk-in shower in Branford?
items.answer: Yes. Tub-to-shower conversions are one of our most requested bathroom renovations in Branford. The project typically involves removing the existing tub, modifying the drain location, installing waterproofing membrane, and building a tiled walk-in shower with glass enclosure. Most conversions take 2-3 weeks and cost between $12,000 and $25,000 depending on tile selections and shower configuration.
